description: Classify an imaging report that also references a follow-up prescription and lab values. The dominant category is imaging; lab values and medication language are present as distractors.

context.report_text:
RADIOLOGY REPORT
Examination: CT Scan Abdomen & Pelvis with IV Contrast
Accession #: RAD-2024-04421
Ordering Physician: Dr. P. Sharma, Emergency Medicine
Patient: Susan K., DOB 1988-11-03, MRN 00319204
Study Date: 2024-03-22 14:35
Radiologist: Dr. E. Mwangi, Board-Certified Radiologist

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Acute onset right lower quadrant abdominal pain for 18 hours. Nausea
present. WBC 11.8 x10^3/uL (mildly elevated on peripheral blood sample
obtained in ED). Rule out appendicitis vs. ovarian pathology.

TECHNIQUE:
Helical CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis was performed following
administration of 100 mL intravenous iohexol contrast (Omnipaque 350).
Axial slices at 3 mm collimation with coronal and sagittal reformats.

FINDINGS:
Appendix: Visualised in its entirety. Measures 5.8 mm in outer diameter
(upper limit of normal). No periappendiceal fat stranding. No appendicolith
identified. No adjacent lymphadenopathy.
Bowel: Non-dilated loops of small and large bowel. No free air. No
evidence of obstruction or intussusception.
Liver/Biliary: Liver parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesion.
No intra- or extrahepatic biliary ductal dilatation. Gallbladder is
normal in size; no gallstones, no pericholecystic fluid.
Spleen: Normal size and attenuation (9.4 cm craniocaudal).
Pancreas: Unremarkable. No ductal dilatation.
Kidneys: Bilateral kidneys enhance symmetrically. No hydronephrosis,
nephrolithiasis, or perinephric stranding.
Gynaecological structures: Uterus is anteverted, normal morphology.
Right ovary: 2.1 cm simple follicular cyst — likely physiological.
Left ovary: Unremarkable. No free fluid in the pelvis.

IMPRESSION:
1. No evidence of acute appendicitis on CT. Appendix calibre borderline;
clinical and laboratory correlation recommended.
2. Simple right ovarian follicular cyst (2.1 cm), likely physiological.
   Gynaecological follow-up advised if symptoms persist.
3. No other acute intra-abdominal or pelvic pathology identified.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
If right lower quadrant pain continues or worsens, surgical and
gynaecological consultation is advised. Patient may take ibuprofen
400 mg PO every 8 hours PRN for pain management pending further evaluation.
Repeat pelvic ultrasound in 6-8 weeks to document resolution of the
follicular cyst is suggested.
